attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who capitalizes on stages where GC contenders neutralize each other. Demonstrates patience and tactical maturity in reading when breaks will succeed.
team_tactics: Insufficient data to establish clear team tactical patterns. Appears to operate independently in breakaway scenarios.
terrain_preference: Stages where GC dynamics create opportunities for breakaways to succeed, particularly when overall contenders are focused on marking each other rather than controlling the race.
vulnerability: Limited observations suggest sprints and pure climbing stages where breakaways are aggressively controlled may not suit his winning profile.
key_patterns: Late-career breakthrough: first Tour stage win after 10 participations indicates experience-based tactical evolution
Capitalizes on GC battle stalemates when favorites neutralize each other in finale
Selects breakaway opportunities with strong probability of success rather than speculative moves
form_trajectory: positive: breakthrough Tour stage win after decade of attempts demonstrates improved race-reading ability and tactical maturity
